regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean briefing for March 12, 2026: Current conditions in San Juan show light east-southeast winds at 6 knots, temperatures near 74°F, and partly cloudy skies with a 35% chance of rain. Expect easterly winds to increase to 12-15 knots through the next three days, with temperatures rising to the low-to-mid 80s and scattered showers mainly over open waters. No marine warnings are in effect; seas remain moderate and stable. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor localized showers near St. Thomas and Antigua, but overall conditions favor safe operations; passengers should carry rain gear for brief afternoon showers while enjoying mostly fair weather in ports including St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Turks & Caicos.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean briefing for March 12, 2026: Cozumel is currently at 79°F with light ESE winds at 4 knots and partly cloudy skies. Expect a shift to more unsettled weather over the next three days with increasing rain chances, including showers and thunderstorms peaking near 70% daily. Southeast winds will strengthen to 6-7 knots before veering northeast by Day 3. Captains operating near Cozumel, Costa Maya, and Belize City should prepare for localized heavy rain and frequent thunderstorm activity, which may reduce visibility and cause rougher seas; avoid exposed anchorages during storms. Cruise passengers should plan outdoor activities early today and remain alert for weather updates as conditions deteriorate by midweek.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region today features light east winds at 5 knots and partly cloudy skies with temperatures near 70°F at Nassau. Expect gradual warming with overcast conditions developing by tomorrow, along with a slight chance of light rain or drizzle—rain chances rise to 34% by day three as southeast winds increase to 15 knots. Mariners should monitor southeast to south-southwest winds for moderate wind shifts and overcast conditions that may reduce visibility near Freeport and Bimini. Cruise and fishing captains should plan for generally safe conditions today but remain alert for damp decks and slower visibility during morning hours on days two and three, especially in anchorage areas around the Berry Islands and Turks & Caicos.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean briefing for March 12, 2026: Aruba reports steady east winds at 15 knots with partly cloudy skies and minimal rain risk today. Expect overcast conditions with east winds increasing to 16-17 knots through March 14, accompanied by a rising chance of scattered showers up to 30 percent. No marine warnings are in effect, but vessel operators should monitor wind increases for potential small craft advisories if sustained winds approach 20 knots. Anchorage in Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena remains sheltered; fishing captains should plan for light chop and intermittent showers later in the period, securing gear accordingly.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions today feature light easterly winds 8 knots and temperatures near 78°F, with partly cloudy skies and a low 13 percent chance of rain at Grand Cayman. Expect a slight uptick in shower activity tomorrow with a 30 percent rain chance and easterly winds near 9 knots, then a return to mainly dry, partly cloudy to overcast skies through Day 3. Ports from Roatan to Costa Maya should continue smooth operations with minimal weather disruptions; however, fishing captains should monitor localized showers tomorrow that may reduce visibility briefly. Anchorage conditions remain stable with no advisories—small craft operators can maintain routine schedules but remain alert for isolated showers, especially near Belize City and Cozumel.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles weather for March 12, 2026: Barbados reports steady ENE winds 15-18 knots with temperatures near 78-81°F, mostly cloudy skies and minimal rain chances through day three. Expect similar trade wind flow across St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada, with occasional overcast conditions but low precipitation probabilities below 20 percent. Mariners should maintain vigilance for choppy seas due to persistent 15-18 kt trades; no Small Craft Advisory is in effect, but smaller vessels should secure loose gear and monitor local updates. Cruise passengers can anticipate generally stable conditions with brief cloud cover; fishing captains should plan for moderate wind-driven seas, especially near exposed anchorages in St. Lucia and Dominica.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles region is clear this morning with light west winds at 1 knot and temperatures near 55°F. Expect daytime highs near 91°F today with light northwest winds around 8 knots and morning fog reducing visibility, especially near Marina del Rey and Long Beach harbors. Over the next two days, expect cooling into the mid-80s and low 70s with overcast skies and light variable winds under 10 knots. Mariners should watch for reduced visibility in early hours and plan for calm seas; fishing captains should use caution with patchy fog at daybreak near Catalina Island, while cruise and yacht operations will enjoy generally safe and stable conditions through the period.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada and surrounding waters will see calm, clear conditions today with light SSW winds at 2 knots and temperatures starting near 56°F, rising to a high near 82°F. Southwest winds will increase slightly to 7 knots tomorrow with overcast skies but no rain expected, followed by similar conditions on day three with a slight chance of isolated sprinkles near Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos. Mariners can expect smooth seas and favorable anchorage conditions at Ensenada Harbor and Punta Banda Cove; fishing captains should plan for stable conditions, though some cloud cover may reduce solar visibility after today. Passengers should dress in layers for cool mornings and warm afternoons, and yacht operators should maintain routine weather checks as light southerly winds persist.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas region weather for March 12, 2026: currently 72°F with light NNE winds at 4 knots under clear skies and zero precipitation. Expect steady conditions over the next three days with light to moderate NW winds increasing to 14 knots by day three; skies will range from clear to overcast, but no rain is forecast. Mariners should note calm seas at all three ports—Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz—with no advisories in effect. Fishing and yacht captains can plan for stable conditions, but monitor wind shifts from NNW to NW as they may affect anchorage comfort in exposed areas. Passengers should enjoy outdoor activities with mild temperatures and minimal wind.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region conditions for March 12, 2026: Clear skies with light NNW winds at 4 knots and no rain expected today. Northwest winds will increase to 10-11 knots over the next three days, with partly cloudy skies and zero precipitation chance. Mariners should expect calm seas and favorable visibility for operations across Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata ports. Fishing captains and yacht operators can plan for smooth conditions; no advisories or warnings are in effect. Passengers should enjoy outdoor activities but remain hydrated and use sun protection during peak daylight hours.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta and surrounding ports including Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala will see light northwest winds today at 4 to 8 knots, with temperatures steady in the upper 70s to low 80s Fahrenheit. Skies will remain partly cloudy to overcast with virtually no rain expected through the next three days. Mariners can expect calm seas and good visibility; no Small Craft Advisories or other marine warnings are in effect. Fishing captains should plan for stable conditions, while yacht operators docking at Punta Mita or anchoring near Yelapa will find sheltered waters and minimal wind impact.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 12, 2026: Barcelona currently steady at 53°F with light northwest winds at 2 knots under overcast skies and a minimal 8% chance of rain. Expect mild westerly winds around 6 knots today with continued cloud cover and low rain risk. Visibility may reduce tomorrow at ports like Valencia and Palma de Mallorca due to fog; mariners should maintain reduced speeds and monitor radar closely. By day three, increasing southeast winds near 10 knots and scattered rain showers around Malaga and Cadiz will demand caution for small craft; secure loose gear and prepare for localized wet conditions.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean briefing for March 12, 2026. Expect overcast skies with light southerly winds 2-5 knots across key ports including Naples, Genoa, Marseille, and Split. Naples shows a 40-43% chance of scattered rain showers today, increasing slightly tomorrow with patchy fog reducing visibility near coastal anchorages from Civitavecchia to Venice. Mariners should maintain vigilant navigation in reduced visibility conditions, especially near constrained harbor entrances. Cruise and yacht operators should prepare for damp decks and slick surfaces; fishing captains advised to monitor local fog trends and adjust schedules accordingly. No marine wind warnings are in effect.
regions.eastern-mediterranean:
Eastern Mediterranean Weather Briefing for March 12, 2026:

Current conditions in Athens/Piraeus show clear skies, light WSW winds at 1 knot, and a temperature of 55°F. Expect light easterly winds around 5 knots with fog developing tomorrow morning, reducing visibility in ports such as Athens, Santorini, and Mykonos. Overcast skies and continued light northerly winds will persist on March 13, with fog returning on March 14; all days remain dry with no rain threat. Mariners should exercise caution when navigating nearshore in areas prone to fog, especially around Piraeus and Rhodes anchorages, and advise cruise and fishing vessels to maintain vigilant watch and use radar due to reduced visibility during early morning hours.
